question: For the quarterly data platform, which interpretation best reflects what can (and cannot) be inferred from the 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: The combined height of all segments in each bar shows overall performance trends across the year.
options.B: Because each bar is normalized to 100%, all three metrics must follow the same trend over time.
options.C: Q3 has the highest number of complaints received in absolute terms compared to the other quarters.
options.D: The chart supports comparing the proportions of metrics within each quarter, but not comparing their absolute quantities across quarters.

explanation: 100% stacking normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking (e.g., one metric > another in every quarter). Invalid inferences include cross-quarter total comparisons or combining unrelated categories.
